inline_comments.message:
you can also use the builder to set the properties.
LockReason.builder().reason(lockReasonEnum).details(details).build();

Have put this in currently, but overall I think setters are more readable, especially as I feel with more properties this builder can just get longer and longer.

Yes this will always be READY

Well composite state is always an empty object in this case?

I set it here so it does not throw a null pointer, then you set the cm handle state within the Composite state

not sure if we should add values arent using yet...
(maybe it's OK)
I think teh try catch block should be around one single cmHandlesync attempt otherwise the watchdog will go to sleep on after first failure although there are many more handles to process
remove enum both form Class as instacneNames (IntelliJ should do this automatically if you used consistent naming)
following I workshop we agreed that teh details should include the timestamp in the detail so we thee retry mechanism (Aditya,s patch)can build in a delay befeor re-attempting...

I think we should have a RegistryPersistecce(Helper) class that implements methods like
(usual reasons, small classes with limited responsibilities, easy to reuse easy to test)

saveSate(cmHandleId, CmHandleState) overloaded with
saveSate(cmHandleId, CmHandleState, LockReason)

and maybe also the related queries like

etc.

Initially, I specified the Enum on the name, as within the composite state complex object we have an inner class already called LockReason which is referenced in the builder to down below, so I wanted to differentiate them.

duplicated outside the try catch block (after you remove break), you can remove this one

move this inside the try block
and this remains outside try try-catch (as is) so the watchdog continues with the next one!

Cm Handle State can be removed because the mapping will be done automatically with the change to the variable name here

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/129322/15/cps-ncmp-service/src/main/java/org/onap/cps/ncmp/api/inventory/CompositeState.java
It will be used to track the number of lock attempts. This will be leveraged by the retry mechanism for priority of which cm handles should be retried
The else handles this as far as I am aware
Has been resolved as part of latest refactor
This test has now been removed with latest refactoring.

NB: I personally think this detail section can be in some structured format instead of a plain string.

For now we are actually using it for recording error message and for the retry attempts.

If we have some structured format then it would be easy to serialize/deserialize and persist and read back.

I know we can also do all sorts of things with strings as well (using pattern matching and stuff) but this comment of mine is purely keeping easability in mind.

What does the team think? (this might not be in this user story scope but if there is value in this we can think about it)
not sure we need all these, see next comment

The exception message is logged as part of the lock reason details. I'm not sure if logging it any further is necessary?

With null or empty? If it's null it just won't come into this section here,
and it will default to the details and reason below. There is a test which covers a null scenario.

I don't think the lock reason can just be empty an empty object currently.

NB:
Normally we don't query the DB to get the information about the cm handles. Just enabling the log level to Trace will help during debug.
log.trace("Failed to update the CM handles state to READY: {}",e.getMessage())

Well the reason is passed in directly through the parameters as an enum

If the details is an empty string it won't match the pattern and will just default to the string below, being Attempt # failed.
